statement.markdown: Given a queue with two elements, implement a merge operation to combine the queue into a single element, while maintaining the order of elements from the original queue. The merge operation should remove the front element from the current queue and add a new element that is the combination of the front and rear elements of the current queue.
statement.notes: Ensure the order of elements is preserved.

hints.text: Consider implementing a custom Queue class with merge and dequeue operations.

editorial.title: Merging Queue Elements
editorial.intuition: To preserve order, create a new queue with a single element, combining the fronts of the original queues.
editorial.optimalSolution: Utilize a custom Queue class to efficiently implement merge and dequeue operations.

buggyStarterCode.python:
def Queue.merge(self, q1, q2):
    q1.deque.append(q2.deque[0]); q2.deque.pop(0); return q1.deque[0] + q1.deque[0]

functionSignature.java: public void merge(Queue q1, Queue q2);
